title: trifolium riograndense subsp. riograndense
intro: Trifolium riograndense subsp. riograndense, also known as the Rio Grande Clover, is a perennial plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae). It is rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Trifolium riograndense subsp. riograndense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
